steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim to illustrate is that May is puzzled by Newland's lack of enthusiasm for Newport. The correct quotation must show May's own confusion or inability to understand his reluctance — not just background facts or Newland's feelings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"The Newport Archery Club always held its August meeting at the Beauforts\'."': A factual detail about the archery club's schedule. Says nothing about May's state of mind. ✗
  • Choice B — '"[May] represented peace, stability, comradeship, and the steadying sense of an unescapable duty."': Describes how Newland perceives May's general character — not her puzzlement about his behavior. ✗
  • Choice C — '"May herself could not understand [Newland\'s] obscure reluctance to fall in with so reasonable and pleasant a way of spending the summer."': "Could not understand" directly expresses May's puzzlement about Newland's reluctance — exactly the claim being illustrated. ✓
  • Choice D — '"[Newland] had the feeling of unexplained excitement with which, on half-holidays at school, he used to start off into the unknown."': Describes Newland's internal feeling, not May's confusion about his behavior. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
